why_not_promoted: 4x sliced visible devices while other jobs occupied GPUs 0 and 1
MORI SDMA hot-weight transport fell back to torch because the visible device set was sliced and nonzero/non-contiguous
profiler-enabled run failed after step 1 with HSA_STATUS_ERROR_OUT_OF_RESOURCES and 122 MB free
trace-export success validates profiler plumbing only; throughput is not comparable

trace_export.fixed_issue: C++ MORI KernelRegistry now prefers ~/.mori/jit/<arch>_<nic>_profiler/latest when ENABLE_PROFILER is set, instead of loading installed non-profiler hsaco first.

read: The profiler-loader path is fixed: compact dispatch/combine traces now export real MORI-VIZ JSON with StandardCompact* slot names. The 4x sliced run is not a throughput comparison and should not be used to rank native compact performance. Its phase windows show receive/count waits dominate the profiled compact collectives, while row staging and receive-copy are much smaller. The next valid performance action is a clean 8x profiler gate on an idle node, followed by a non-profiled sparse 6-step stability gate and then a 20-step promotion candidate if stable.
